name: Swan.Collections.Circular_Buffers

documentation.description:
Simple circular buffer implementation, by default it will prevent
overwrites this can be altered by setting Allow_Overwrite to True,
otherwise a write that would cause an overwrite
will instead raise an Would_Overwrite exception.
Checks if the circular buffer contains any unread items.
documentation.returns: True if the buffer is empty, False otherwise.

subprograms.name: Read_Item
subprograms.code: function Read_Item (CB : in out Circular_Buffer) return T

documentation.description:
Gets the least recent inserted item in the buffer, this is guaranteed
as long as the buffer is in no overwrite mode, otherwise FIFO ordering
cannot be ensured.

parameters.name: CB
parameters.description: the circular buffer to operate on

exceptions.name: Is_Empty
exceptions.description: may be raised in case there is no fresh data.

subprograms.name: Read_Item_Unchecked
subprograms.code: function Read_Item_Unchecked (CB : in out Circular_Buffer) return T

documentation.description:
# SAFETY NOTE:
This function might return data that was previously read, only use
if you keep track of previously seen data and want to avoid a call
to Is_Empty or handling the possible exception.

parameters.name: CB
parameters.description: the circular buffer to operate on

documentation.returns: One item of type T; possibly previously read data.

subprograms.name: Write_Item
subprograms.code: procedure Write_Item (CB : in out Circular_Buffer; Item : T)

documentation.description: Insert an item into the circular buffer

parameters.name: CB
parameters.description: the circular buffer to operate on

exceptions.name: Would_Overwrite
exceptions.description:
may be raised if the insertion
would cause an overwrite of data and Allow_Overwrite is False (default)
